app.title: dev-dashboard
watchlist.empty: Add tickets or PRs to your watchlist.
repoBrowser.title: Browse Repos
repoBrowser.placeholder: Search repos by name or owner...
prBrowser.title: Browse PRs
prBrowser.noRepos: No repos configured. Add some in GitHub settings first.
prBrowser.noPRs: No open PRs in this repo.
watchedPRs.title: Watched PRs
watchedPRs.empty: No PRs in watchlist yet. Use "Browse PRs" to add some.
viewRepos.title: Configured Repos
viewRepos.empty: No repos configured. Use "Browse repos" to add some.
settings.title: Settings
settings.ghEmail.hint: Used to filter PRs to only yours. This is the email on your commits.
settings.ghUser.hint: Only needed if you want notifications when someone requests your review.
settings.jiraProxy: Proxy URL (optional, if Jira blocks direct CORS)
settings.jiraProxy.hint: Jira Cloud doesn't allow direct browser calls (CORS). If the Jira panel fails, you need a small proxy (Cloudflare Worker/Vercel function) to forward the request with your auth. Leave empty to try direct first.
settings.exportPasswordPrompt: Set a password to encrypt the file (leave empty for plain JSON):
settings.importPasswordPrompt: Enter the decryption password (leave empty if not encrypted):
export.title: Export Settings
export.legend: Categories with data are checked by default. Uncheck anything you don't want to include. Credentials (tokens and keys) are included in their respective category.
import.title: Import Settings
import.noData: No importable data found in file.
import.encryptedNoPassword: File is encrypted. Enter the decryption password.
import.noChanges: No changes — already up to date.
help.title: Settings Guide
help.github.title: GitHub
help.github.token: Personal access token with 'repo' scope. Used to fetch PRs, check runs, and reviews from your repos. Generate one at github.com > Settings > Developer settings > Personal access tokens.
help.github.repos: The repos to monitor. Use 'Browse repos' to search and select them, or 'View repos' to see and remove configured ones. Format: owner/repo.
help.github.email: The email associated with your GitHub commits. Used to filter PRs so you only see yours. This is the email you have configured in git.
help.github.username: Your GitHub username (optional). Only needed if you want browser notifications when someone requests your review on a PR.
help.jira.title: Jira
help.jira.domain: Your Atlassian domain, e.g. yourcompany.atlassian.net. Do not include https://.
help.jira.credentials: Your Jira email and API token. Generate a token at id.atlassian.com > Manage API tokens.
help.jira.jql: Custom JQL query for the 'My Jira' panel. Defaults to showing your unresolved tickets. Only change if you want a different filter.
help.jira.proxy: Jira Cloud blocks direct browser requests (CORS). If the Jira panel fails, deploy a small proxy worker to forward requests with your auth. Leave empty to try direct first.
help.watchlist.title: Watchlist
help.watchlist.jira: Manually add Jira ticket keys (e.g. PROJ-123) separated by commas. These are always visible in the Watchlist panel.
help.watchlist.prs: Pin specific PRs to your watchlist. Use 'Browse PRs' to find and pin them, or 'View watched PRs' to see and unpin. Watched PRs show CI status and approval badges.
help.io.title: Import / Export
help.io.export: Export your settings to a JSON file. You can select which categories to include and optionally encrypt with a password.
help.io.import: Import settings from a file. You'll see a preview of what will change before applying. Select which categories to import to avoid overwriting existing data.
ai.modal.title: AI Insights
ai.empty: No insights yet. Click Generate to start.
ai.error: Could not generate insights. Check your API key in Settings.
ai.noKey: Configure your AI provider in Settings to use this feature.
settings.aiApiKey.hint: Your API key for the selected provider.
settings.aiCustomUrl.hint: Base URL for chat completions. Cloud: https://your-api.com/v1 — Local: localhost:11434/v1
